summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a wildly praised 1974 show with standout versions of Playing in the Band, Eyes of the World and Wharf Rat.
review: The recording is a clean sound‑board mix that lets the band’s tight interplay shine. Reviewers rave about the second‑set jam marathon – especially Playing in the Band, Eyes of the World, Wharf Rat and Sugar Magnolia – while also noting superb piano work from Keith and strong vocal harmonies with Donna. Overall the performance is described as energetic and inspired, though a couple of brief, non‑specific reviews keep the sentiment mixed.

ai_rating.rationale: Two detailed 5‑star reviews highlight an outstanding musical performance and high‑quality soundboard recording, while two lower‑rated generic reviews temper the overall consensus.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear, balanced mix with minimal crowd bleed; reviewers note it does the show justice compared to other noisy mixes.

standout_songs: It Must Have Been the Roses
show_quality.setlist_flow: Set one is described as tight and compact, while set two launches into an extended improvisational marathon that maintains energy and momentum throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Rowdy, laid‑back guitar work with strong vocal delivery; praised for his licks and interplay.
band_member_comments.Phil: Heavy‑duty bass lines, fretting on the low end, tight partnership with drums.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith's piano/keyboard work is called 'freaky' and excellent counterpoint throughout the set.
band_member_comments.Drums: Billy Kreutzmann drives the rhythm, tearing into the drums and locking in with Phil.
